instructions: (Claude Code's built-in system prompt; see --bare mode docs)

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a real-codebase bug in `berry-1` (project `berry`, an open-source C/C++ project from the BugsC++ corpus).

The buggy source tree is on the host at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/berry-1/berry/buggy-1` and is also mounted at `/work` inside a Linux/amd64 container named `bench-t4-8e82c27f2e0340a0bbf1` (gdb, the buggy binary, and all build deps live in that container; the binary is NOT runnable on this host directly).

Buggy binary inside the container: `/work/berry`
Failing test invocation:           `./berry tests/bitwise.be`
Observed behavior:                  `no_observation`
Bug type:                          `other`
Language:                          `c`

How to investigate
------------------
* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/berry-1/berry/buggy-1` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).
* To RUN the binary, run gdb, or execute anything else inside the build environment, use Bash with this template:

      docker exec -i -w /work bench-t4-8e82c27f2e0340a0bbf1 bash -c '<cmd>'

  e.g.
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-8e82c27f2e0340a0bbf1 bash -c 'ls -la'
      docker exec -w /work bench-t4-8e82c27f2e0340a0bbf1 bash -c 'gdb -batch -ex run -ex bt --args ./berry tests/bitwise.be'

  The container is dedicated to this case; it'll be torn down when this session ends, so don't worry about cleanup.

Final answer
------------
Identify the root cause in the source, propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>
  LOCAL FIX:  <minimal code change>
  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

Do NOT modify the source files on disk. Just investigate and produce the diagnosis as your final assistant message.

* To READ source code, use Read / Grep / your normal tools — the source tree is at `/Users/ibraheemamin/projects/COS484/chatdbgpro/data/workspaces/berry-1/berry/buggy-1` (it's been added via `--add-dir`).

Final answer
------------

Your final response MUST include three labelled paragraphs:

  ROOT CAUSE: <file:line and what is wrong>

  GLOBAL FIX: <structural change preventing this CLASS of bug>

queries.response:
ROOT CAUSE: `src/be_vm.c:746` — the `FLIP` opcode (bitwise NOT, `~`) for integer operands incorrectly executes arithmetic negation: `var_setint(dst, -a->v.i)` instead of bitwise complement `~a->v.i`. So at runtime `~a` where `a=11` produces `-11` rather than `-12`, causing `assert(~a == -12)` at `tests/bitwise.be:13` to fail. (The literal case `~11` is folded at compile time via `be_code.c:609` using `~e->v.i`, which is correct — confirming the operator's intended semantics and that the VM path is the buggy one.)

LOCAL FIX: In `src/be_vm.c` inside `opcase(FLIP)`, change `var_setint(dst, -a->v.i);` to `var_setint(dst, ~a->v.i);` so the integer fast path performs bitwise complement, matching the constant-folding in `be_code.c` and the opcode's documented semantics (`R(A) <- ~RK(B)` per `src/be_opcodes.h:28`).

GLOBAL FIX: This class of bug — copy/paste between the structurally identical `NEG` and `FLIP` opcase blocks where only the operator differs — is exactly what unit/regression tests on each VM opcode should catch, but more robustly the duplication itself should be eliminated. Define a single macro/inline helper such as `VM_INT_UNARY(op, intexpr, instr_sym)` that takes the integer expression and the instance-method symbol, and instantiate it for `NEG` (`-a->v.i`, `"-*"`) and `FLIP` (`~a->v.i`, `"~"`). This removes the opportunity for the integer branch and the instance-dispatch symbol to drift apart, and makes adding future unary ops a one-line declaration. Pair this with a CI test that exercises every opcode listed in `be_opcodes.h` against both integer and instance operands so semantic regressions in any single arm are caught immediately.

tool_calls.call: docker exec -w /work bench-t4-8e82c27f2e0340a0bbf1 bash -c './berry tests/bitwise.be; echo exit=$?'
